top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 89, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
GFNY Aguascalientes' mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 89 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Aguascalientes' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
55ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
When someone attacks on mountain passes, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 89 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Aguascalientes. Train it until it feels boring.
At GFNY Aguascalientes, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Aguascalientes, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mountain passes at tempo for 89 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 89 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.heat: March in Isla San Marcos, Aguascalientes, Mexico can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before GFNY Aguascalientes. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Aguascalientes’s 89 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GFNY Aguascalientes. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY Aguascalientes requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mountain passes to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 89 miles of mountain passes in Isla San Marcos, Aguascalientes, Mexico.
